title: Protocol for Conducting Multilingual Customizable Structured Electronic Brainwriting Session: Utilizing Google Sheets with Google Apps Script
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3hmul","text":"This protocol outlines the process for using Google Sheets, along with Google Apps Script and Google Colab, to set up and manage a language-localizable Multilingual Customizable Structured Electronic Brainwriting (mCSEB) session, which enables remote collaborative sessions for idea generation and downstream analysis. This technique creates an online platform by automating traditional brainwriting sessions, which involve six participants each coming up with three ideas in five minutes per round and passing their paper round-robin to the next participant, building new ideas upon previous contributions over the course of six rounds, producing 108 ideas in just half an hour.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"786v6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nbhv","text":"Google Sheets functions as a versatile platform for real-time input collection and documentation while enabling participants to effortlessly browse the spreadsheet and contribute to the idea development process.  With this approach, moderators can efficiently guide and document brainstorming sessions, whether they’re in-person or virtual. The (6-)6-3-5 structured electronic brainwriting system is designed to automatically track progress, organize participant contributions, and prepare input for further analysis in Google Colab, which offers access to various statistical and machine learning libraries of the Python language.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"9l5eh","text":"Prerequisites:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bf40d","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"al0t6","text":"Basic spreadsheet skills","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5lev0","text":"Familiarity with Google Workspace and JavaScript","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bu16s","text":"Moderator access in Google Workspace (if you're in an organization)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"clbi6","text":"Research the Focus Area:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9vvj9","text":"Before kicking off the brainwriting session, take some time to dive deep into your research question or focus area. It's really important to have a solid grasp of the topic and what you hope to achieve in the session.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"78pp6","text":"Put forward a clear and straightforward problem statement. This will help participants get engaged and stay focused on coming up with new ideas.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b6kkl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"54dbg","text":"Prepare for Implementation:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":26}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f2nc6","text":"Create a customized script using the Structured Electronic Brainwriting Session Configuration Generator tool.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cnc76","text":"You can familiarize yourself with the Google Apps Script, make sure you understand the script provided, and make any necessary changes to fit your requirements. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6g8fu","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5dorq","text":"Test the Setup:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cs09p","text":"Make sure to tackle any bugs or limitations you find before you work with your actual participants. Conduct a trial run with a small group or colleagues to identify any potential issues with the script, permissions, or workflow. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f0658","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"739pr","text":"Ensure Participant Readiness:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":28}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4nk2c","text":"Let participants know what to expect from the session ahead of time. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4jj4a","text":"Make sure everyone has a stable internet connection and can access Google Sheets without any issues.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"1hkph","text":"Data Privacy and Security:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fgjsa","text":"Let participants know that their inputs are visible to others since six participants access the same Google Sheet simultaneously. This is by design to help spark new ideas and let participants get inspired from one another.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bvpkv","text":"If anonymity is desired, make sure to strip any identifiable details before you share or analyze the responses. Using codenames for worksheets can help safeguard identities and enhance privacy.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"44ei7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9au1a","text":"Script Permissions:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"filsv","text":"Google Apps Script needs certain permissions to access and make changes to Google Sheets. Before running the script (see Step 9 and Step 29), users will be asked to approve these permissions. It's a good idea for users to check the script to see how it works and make sure it meets their needs.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6p68d","text":"Like any custom code, this script might have some bugs.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"52rv4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5jtii","text":"Internet Connectivity:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f8v5h","text":"To keep the script running smoothly, a steady internet connection is a must since it needs real-time access to Google Sheets. If the connection drops, you might face delays, missing data, or even lost inputs. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"13aes","text":"Make sure everyone involved in the brainwriting session has a reliable internet connection to avoid these problems.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"5lqll","text":"Computer with internet access","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"47le1","text":"Google account with access to:","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3keu1","text":"Google Sheets,","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6c42g","text":"Google Apps Script (enabled),","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":29}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"enrb7","text":"Web browser - Google Chrome (recommended), Firefox (supported), Safari (supported), Microsoft Edge (supported),","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":111}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"87c9l","text":"Google Colaboratory (enabled)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":29}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bk9oc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dolpl","text":"No additional software installation is needed.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Open Google Sheets ( sheets.google.com ) Google Sheets Google LLC Script generator directs users to Google Sheets with a link as well
Create a new blank spreadsheet Start a new blank spreadsheet
Access to Apps Script Google Apps Script Google
Paste the generated script to Apps Script
Save the script with a descriptive name (e.g., "brainwriting-session") Rename project and check GLOBAL_VARIABLES
Accept any required permissions when prompted
Run the script
Click on Extensions > Apps Script Access to Apps Script from the spreadsheet
Give spreadsheet a meaningful name (i.e. Brainwriting Session) Change the spreadsheet name
A new window with default configuration will open Apps Script file with the default function
After "Run" the script, "Authorization required" pop-up window appears. Click on "Review permission" Authorize the script - Review permission
Choose the related account. Authorize the script - Choose Account
Advanced verificationSince this is a custom script, it will warn about code verification. Click on "Advanced" Authorize the script - Advanced verification
Click on "Go to {script-name} (unsafe)" Authorize the script - Go to the session

Allow accessAnd "Allow" the script to create the brainwriting session. In case a logo visualization is selected, there might be multiple boxes to select. "Select all" and "Continue". Otherwise, just Authorize the script - Allow access
Notice that the "Run" command is inactive. Be sure to "Save project to Drive" to activate the "Run" command. Save project to Drive
To activate the Navigation Menu, refresh the spreadsheet. Refresh the browser Navigation Menu activation by refreshing the page
Start the session when all participants are ready in their designated sheets.
Participants write 3 ideas within the time limit and click on the check box. When a participant finishes the number of ideas for the round, checking the box below signals readiness to the moderator
While running the script, participants will receive a message declaring the session's start. Notice the yellow warning when the script is running, start the timer when the automation stops.
Start the session with Workshop Tools > Start Session Click "Start Session"

When the moderator submits the round, the ideas from each sheet are added to the ProgressTracking sheet. Previous round values are swapped round-robin to the next participant's sheet. Round 1 values are collected and next round started

Participant sheets are updated with the previous answers (grey background), and a new check box appears under the current round column ideas (green background). Next participant's (language=de) sheet in the second round, see regardless of the language of other participants, round 1 ideas are translated to German for a smoother experience
Idea Generation and Submission
Repeat idea generation and collection steps for each round until the end of Round 6. go to step #16 Ideas are collected, and the last round ideas are ready to be submitted
(Optional) Export collected ideas, download in the preferred format (Optional) Download ideas in Excel format: File > Download > Microsoft Excel (.xlsx)
Brief participants about the session
Determine the session date and time

Send an email with: Session date and time Time expectations per round Problem statement/topic Basic instructions for using the sheet Their assigned starting sheet The session participation link that is copied in Step 13.2 Any technical requirements Contact information for technical support

Use the following template to instruct participants: Basic Instructions for Using the 6-3-5 Electronic Brainwriting Sheet 1. Accessing Your Sheet Open the link sent to your email Navigate to your assigned sheet 2. Round Progression You will have one column for your ideas per round Write one complete idea per row Each round lasts [5] minutes When the time is up, the moderator will activate "Next Round" Your sheet will be updated to show new ideas to build upon Complete all three rows in your new column Check the box in the Progress Tracking sheet when you finish 3. Important Rules Do not delete or modify others' ideas Wait for the moderator's signal before starting each round If you have technical issues, contact the moderator immediately Complete all three ideas before checking your progress box
Invite all participants to the spreadsheet
Click the "Share" button in the top right corner Click "Share" button
Delete the default function.
Share results with participants (Optional)
Now the "Run" command is active, "Run" the script "Run" the script

Go to the Structured Electronic Brainwriting Session Configurator tool Structured Electronic Brainwriting Session Configuration Generat 0.1 Mahmut Berkan Çetin Note. Streamlit Cloud automatically puts inactive web apps to sleep. If this happens, simply wake the app, which usually takes about 30 seconds to start up again.

Fill in the "Quick Setup" form in accordance with the research needs, a (6-)6-3-5 Structured Electronic Brainwriting session is given by default, and click on the "Generate Script" button. Fill in the form and generate the script
Choose the application interface and session language (Default: English). Turkish, German, Spanish, and French languages are already localized. Choose app language
Conduct preparatory research.

Preparatory steps before using Customized Structured Electronic Brainwriting techniques: Define the Research Question or Objective Ensure it is exploratory or ideation-focused (not hypothesis-driven). Conduct a Needs Assessment Clarify the desired outcome: idea generation, problem reframing, or innovation. Analyze Participant Characteristics Identify target users or stakeholders (personas, demographics, expertise). Consider cognitive diversity, cultural norms, and power dynamics. Evaluate Contextual and Logistical Constraints Check for digital access, language requirements, and time zones (for remote sessions). Address ethical considerations (consent, anonymity, equity). Choose the Brainwriting Variant Decide between 6-3-5, freewriting, digital rotation, or asynchronous formats. Note. Some users may need several days to complete the preparatory research, so this step serves as a checklist once they have reached their decision.
Copy the code to the clipboard, and/or download the script for archival purposes. Copy code by clicking on the icon at the right upper corner (invisible until hovering over it)
Create Colab Environment Google Colab Google Llc

Last round submission automatically fetches data, but stops generating further input from the participants. Participants then receive a message thanking their participation in the CSEB session. A "Session Completed" message will appear to participants. Session end message to participants for their contribution
Verify all rounds are completed
(OPTIONAL) If the language of the session is other than the given languages, choose "Localization" tab for translating the user-visible UI related part of the script.
Activate multilingual session for mCSEB (Default: mono-lingual session, CSEB) Toggle the Multilingual Session option
Execution completed without an error Execution completed
To automatically create a landing page, click on "Create Landing Page"
The landing page is automatically created. The landing page shows brief information about the session

The session creation is the next step that automatically creates participant private worksheets and moderator dashboard Workshop Tools > Create Session to automatically create the session items 6 sheets per participant for idea generation 1 tracking sheet Checkbox-based progress tracking Participant sheets (for 6 participants) and one tracking sheet Empty participant sheet example (language=en) Empty participant sheet example (language=tr)
In case there is a logo link added, access should be given in order for function at cell L19 to display the logo Notice that the function fails to display the logo, "Allow access" to the connection
Grant access for Anonymous contributions Choose "Anyone with the link" for anonymous session generation Give "Editor" access to the participants, and copy the link
If an anonymous session is preferred, assign participants anonymous names.
Meanwhile, the moderator checks completion in Progress Tracking The moderator submits the answers and proceeds with the next round Submit the answers using Workshop Tools > Submit & Next.
After the session, it is good practice to restrict the session participation to prevent unintended losses. Change the Editor role to Viewer ==> Share > Role > Viewer
In a new worksheet, copy ideas in a structured format for translation and further analysis by using the "Prepare Data" automation "Prepare Data" for the downstream analysis

"PrepData" worksheet is automatically created, All participant-generated ideas are in "RawIdeas" column Regardless of their language, all were translated into English in the "Translation" column (Optional) Notice that "ManualCategories" column is created for researcher to fill with thematic analysis but it is not mandatory for further analysis. "PrepData" worksheet fetches "IdeaID", "IdeaTimestamp", "RoundTimestamp", "RawIdea", and "Translation" data automatically and structures in an analysis-ready format. Optionally, "ManualCategories" column can be used to write researcher-generated categories.
Click "Workshop Tools" > "Create Colab Environment" to start the downstream analysis Click "Workshop Tools" > "Create Colab Environment"

In the pop-up window, copy the automatically generated session information. Then click on the link with an analysis-ready Colab notebook. Pop-up window with session information and Colab notebook link
Clicking the link opens the notebook directly from GitHub. Notice that it is not saved to Drive. An analysis-ready Colab notebook is opened
Save Colab notebook to Drive in order to prevent unexpected losses.
"Save a copy in Drive" "File" > "Save a copy in Drive"
A copy of the Colab notebook is created and will be opened in a new tab DataAnalysis notebook is saved to Drive
Fill in the Author info for personalization Fill in the Author info
Paste the session information copied in Step 23.2 to the notebook's 2nd section, Data Loading & Configuration, instead of the placeholder information Session information placeholder

(Optional) If there are manual categories and/or sub-categories, the notebook's 2.1. section has the option to include them in the analysis. Use check boxes to activate. Furthermore, the analysis by default uses K-means clustering categories for the analysis, it can be changed to BERTopic-generated labels. Additional details to be filled if manual categories/sub-categories are added and if a more advanced topic labeling is desired
Now that the required information is given, the notebook can run all analyses Click "Run all" to start the analysis
After the Python libraries are installed, it will ask for access to the Google Sheets Click "Allow" for Colab acces to Google Sheets
Choose an account Choose the account that allows the access
Continue further authorization questions Click "Continue" for authorization
Give access to Google Sheets "Choose all" options and "Continue"
Review the code before using. All methods and code snippets are explained in casual and academic language. Please be sure to use the notebook after a careful review.

Add domain-specific synonyms in section 3 of the notebook. The synonym map can be iteratively changed based on the desired results. An example of a synonym map for the Corporate Local Responsibility (COLOR) related session

After granting access, the notebook will quickly run the whole analysis Colab completes the analysis successfully Each analysis displays the results in figures or tables Tables are written back to the Google Sheet (i.e., Brainwriting Session)
Check section 6 for UMAP projections of participant ideas UMAP projections scatterplot
Check section 8 for Cosine similarity heatmap between categories Cosine similarity heatmap

Check section 9 for Keyword Co-occurrence Network Keyword co-occurrence network (threshold=3) If the network is too dense or too sparse, adjust the threshold parameter Keyword co-occurrence network (threshold=2)
Check section 10 for the list of the most frequent keywords for each category Category keyword frequency table
Check section 11 for BERTopic categories and visuals related to these categories BERTopic categories table

Check automatically added worksheets to the Google Sheets. processing-labels worksheet writes back the processed text in each step of cleaning and the labels of K-means clustering df_keywords_[mac] writes back the table of frequencies

(Optional) Each section gives a description of the method and references related to the technique. Although some methods and visuals are self-explanatory, the best practice is to learn more about them and improve the analysis.

(Optional) Automated methods present one side of the story, but for a more comprehensive view, it is advised to use thematic analysis to find categories and sub-categories of the ideas; then, the analysis can be redone with them.

(Optional) With a pre-configured and pre-access-given notebook, it takes less than a minute to get the results. Hence, after-session opinion sharing with participants can be done using the analysis results.
(Optional) Use custom methods and techniques to get the most out of the data and share the notebook with the community for collaboration and open science.
Choose participant languages Toggle to unlock participant name and language customization Note. Some users noted that language selection for participants sometimes require double-selection.
(OPTIONAL) Add a logo for the landing page Tick the "Include a logo in Landing Page?" box and paste an image URL
creator: Mahmut Berkan Çetin
